medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A 2-month history of progressive dysphagia, without hoarseness. Iohexol swallowing revealed a malignant-appearing structure in the inferior-thoracic region. Endoscopic assessment revealed a lesion of mucosal hyperplasia forming a luminal stenosis 36cm from the incisor teeth.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension for the last 9 years, diagnosed with type 2 diabetes over 1 year ago. No prior malignant disease or distant metastases.
medical_history.social_history: Smoked 60 cigarettes per day for 40 years and had been a heavy alcohol drinker for 40 years.

physical_examination.general_examination: Neck and supraclavicular lymph nodes were not palpable. No significant abnormal values were detected in the blood count or in serum and biochemical analysis.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Endoscopic assessment revealed a lesion of mucosal hyperplasia forming a luminal stenosis 36cm from the incisor teeth. Biopsy of the lesion demonstrated an esophageal MEC. On computed tomography (CT) scanning, the tumor was deemed to be resectable.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Biopsy of the lesion under endoscopy
laboratory_examinations.findings: Demonstrated a mucoepidermoid carcinoma of the esophagus
Histological characteristics typical of an esophageal MEC, positive for p-63 and CK5/6, mucus-secreting cells identified using Periodic Acid-Schiff staining

radiographic_examinations.findings: Revealed a malignant-appearing structure in the inferior-thoracic region
Revealed a lesion of mucosal hyperplasia forming a luminal stenosis 36cm from the incisor teeth
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: No evidence of recurrence at 17, 19, and 31 months after surgery

radiographic_examinations.findings: The tumor was deemed to be resectable
Revealed that the wall of esophagus was evenly thickened
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Further CT scans at 19 and 31 months after surgery also showed a thickened esophageal wall

other_tests.findings: Neck and supraclavicular lymph nodes were not palpable

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: Biopsy of the lesion under endoscopy demonstrated a mucoepidermoid carcinoma of the esophagus. Histopathological analysis of the tumor revealed histological characteristics typical of an esophageal MEC, including epidermoid cells arranged in nests, keratin pearl formation, and columnar mucus-secreting cells. Immunohistochemistry results confirmed the diagnosis with positive staining for p-63 and CK5/6.
